### Slide 1

What Doesn't Kill Us: How Freezing Water, Extreme Altitude, and Environmental Conditioning Will Renew Our Lost Evolutionary Strength By: Scott Carney and Wim Hof

### Slide 2

GrowYourAgency Community Cliff Notes: Main Takeaways: Breath - Breathing allows us to bring ourselves back to centre. Stillness - The biggest blessing in life you can ever have is a feeling of stillness, no amount of money or success can ever beat this feeling. Uncomfort - Cold showers, Ice baths and breath holds. Master these three things and you’re a lot closer to mastering yourself. Legal: This resource is purely for educational purposes. All credit s go towards the author. Self education is everything. {29CE4D56-B56C-4501-85BE-4F3C5D055A2F} Hof tells us that the quick breathing adds oxygen to our blood supply so that, at least until we use it up, we won’t have to rely on the air in our lungs to survive. The autonomic urge to gasp for air is based on the mind’s ordinary programming: No air in the lungs means it’s time to breathe. My nervous system has not yet realized there is still oxygen in my bloodstream.* The heavy breathing lets me trick my nervous system into doing things that it isn’t evolutionarily designed for. In other words, I am hacking my body. Instead, I focus on the point behind my ears that Hof says will help activate my brown fat and send waves of heat through my body. Interesting reminder for when I do ice-baths I imitate what I witnessed Hof do in the sauna. With my muscles clenched and my mind focused, it isn’t long before I start to sweat. A thin, steamy mist wafts upward from the group. A skier stops to take pictures. A ski patrolman on a snowmobile pauses to see if we were okay. A snowboarder lets out a shocked cry and speeds by as we plod forward to the summit. ‘ With my muscles clenched and my mind focused, it isn’t long before I start to sweat’ “The only two animals in the world that suffer chronic obesity are humans and the pets we keep at home,” he says. “There’s a connection.” I love this, so true. Self education is everything. {29CE4D56-B56C-4501-85BE-4F3C5D055A2F} The key to fixing the problem, according to Cronise, is to artificially reintroduce seasons back into our lives. Drawing on the growing literature on brown fat and metabolic analysis, he prescribed himself daily hour-long walks in sub-60 degree temperatures along with moderate exercise and calorie restriction. In the course of just 6 months, from June to November, he dropped almost 40 pounds. Try running shirtless in the winter in London? The Wim Hof Method tasks students to hold their breath until they can’t take it anymore. And then hold it just a little longer. This is the quickest and probably safest way to build your own wedge. ‘The wedge’ Start while lying with your back on the floor, then do one more cycle of about 40 breaths. Make the last 10 even a little faster. Then take a giant breath in, turn over, and immediately start doing pushups while holding your breath. Try not to think about anything else other than the count. Or, even better, think of nothing at all and have someone else count for you. You will feel that these were the easiest pushups you’ve ever done, and most likely will be able to smash through the pushup baseline that you set earlier. And you’ll have done it without actually breathing. This will work with just about any sort of strength exercise—pullups, situps, weights, leg lifts, or dips—if for some reason pushups aren’t your thing. Even though everyone’s physiology is different, Hof says that at 3 minutes you’ve cracked into your sympathetic nervous system. 3 minute breath hold is a good sign you’re cracking the method. Self education is everything. {29CE4D56-B56C-4501-85BE-4F3C5D055A2F} “Breathing lets you go deep,” he says to me later, describing how his 20-minute morning routine of deep breathing and breath-holding has led him to places he never expected he would reach. “Sometimes I’ll see a face looking back at me and I know that it’s my own soul. At other times it feels like I’m floating above my body looking down at myself meditating.” We are joined by one of his trainers, a man also named Brian. MacKenzie prefers a regimen of 30 to 45 breaths followed by long holds with loud tribal music filling the studio. Quote From The Book Iman’s Notes Your Thoughts
